name: Ernie Wilkins
description: American jazz saxophonist, bandleader and music arranger
abstract: Ernest Brooks Wilkins Jr. (July 20, 1922 – June 5, 1999) was an American jazz saxophonist, conductor and arranger who spent several years with Count Basie.

concerts.label: Clark Terry and His Big Band

works.t: Blues All Day, Blues All Night

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Count Basie and His Orchestra

works.t: Black, Brown and Beige: Come Sunday
If you could see me now

works_created.t: Blues All Day, Blues All Night

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Burrell
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ‘Cause you said Ernie, so that threw me, I was thinking Ernie Wilkins, I know it’s not Ernie Wilkins.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Burrell — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: Ernie and Jimmy Wilkins were both in the band. They recognized me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: The arrangers who were in the band, namely Thad Jones, Frank Wess and myself, Ernie Wilkins while he was still there... definitely had an advantage being in the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 7, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: In the early years in the Basie Band, we had probably the best jazz arrangers in the business. Ernie Wilkins in a premier arranger of jazz.

about_quotes.speaker: Hayes Pillars
about_quotes.evidence: We had Ernie Wilkins and his brother playing in the band at this time.

about_quotes.speaker: Louie Bellson
about_quotes.evidence: Ernie Wilkins was big with the Basie band... I had all of them writing something during Pearl’s 15 television shows... How am I going to fight with somebody like Blue Mitchell or Conte Candoli or Pete Christlieb or Ernie Wilkins? They all know what to do.
about_quotes.doc_title: Louie Bellson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
